english: Pers., Hind. چادر shawl, plaid, cloak, toga W. -

wylie: tsa na, tsa nas, tsa la

english: more corr. ཅ་ཎ་ཀ་ (ca Na ka) Ssk., chick-pea, Cicer arietinum.
*tsa-rág zér-če* (tsa rag zer ce) Ld. to crackle, of fire, breaking twigs etc.
1. W. curled, frizzled, as hair and similar things. - 2. Lex.: Ssk. meat-offering to the manes.
W. the black mark in a target, tság-ge-la gyob (tsag ge la gyob) hit the mark!

sandal-tree, Sirium myrtifolium, sandal-wood, used for elegant buildings, images of the gods, perfumes, medicines Glr., Med.; in different varieties: dkár-po (dkar po), dmár-po (dmar po) etc., also of fabulous kinds: tsán-dan sbrúl-gyi snyíṅ-po, gór-ši-ša, gláṅ-mgo (tsan dan sbrul gyi snying po, gor shi sha, glang mgo) Glr., Dzl.; fig. something superior in its kind, pha tsán-dan pu-nu mí-lag-tu soṅ (pha tsan dan pu nu mi lag tu song) the elder and younger sons of a distinguished father perform menial services

wylie: tsab tsub, tsab tsob, rtsab rtsub
english: hurry, haste Cs., tsab-tsúb-čan (tsab tsub can) hasty Cs.; tsab-tsúb mi bya (tsab tsub mi bya) Lex. take your time, don't be in a hurry! rtsab-rtsub-méd-par (rtsab rtsub med par) not flitting, like a butterfly, from one object to another Mil.; tsub - liṅ (tsub ling) Sch. hastily, in a hurry (?) - rtsab-hrál (rtsab hral) Lex.; Sch. a loose, dissolute course of life (?) - rtsáb-pa (rtsab pa) Sch. to hurry, to hasten (?).
1. a kind of salt, tsabs-ru-tsá (tsabs ru tsa) S.g. - 2. a tube of horn Sch.

mostly affixed as an enclitic, = snyed (snyed) (sometimes carelessly for tsám-pa (tsam pa) or tsám-du (tsam du)) I. in a relative sense, 1. as much as ₀di-tsam ('di tsam) as much as this, = so much, so many; mi ₀di tsam gsód-pa (mi 'di tsam gsod pa) to kill so many men Glr.; dé-tsam (de tsam) id.; also emphat.: čos de tsam žig bšád-nas (chos de tsam zhig bshad nas) after having given you so much religious instruction Mil.; by way of exclamation: či-tsam (ci tsam) how much! W. and B., či-tsam byas (ci tsam byas) how much have you not done! Glr.; ji-tsam ... dé-tsam (ji tsam ... de tsam) how much... so much (as much as) Cs. - 2. denoting comparison, as to size, degree, intensity, like, as-as, so-as, so that: ri-ráb tsam (ri rab tsam) like Sumeru (in height) Cs; yuṅs-₀bru tsam (yungs 'bru tsam) as big as a grain of mustard-seed; *de ri tón-po tsam dug dé-tsogs di yaṅ yod* (de ri ton po tsam dug de tsogs di yang yod) W. as high as yon mountain is also this one; pús-mo núb-pa tsam (pus mo nub pa tsam) even to sinking in up to the knees (knee-deep); nyi-ma ₀grib-pa tsam (nyi ma 'grib pa tsam) so much that the sun was darkened Glr.; mťai rgyál-po yaṅ dbáṅ-du ₀dús-pa tsam byuṅ (mtha'i rgyal po yang dbang du 'dus pa tsam byung) he became so (powerful), that he could also subdue - or could have subdued - the neighbouring kings Glr. - 3. denoting contingency and restriction: perhaps, if need be, almost, only, but, all but: tsab ruṅ tsám-mo (tshab rung tsam mo) Wdṅ. this may perhaps be used instead, this may, if need be, supply its place; btaṅ-na nam-mǩai bya yaṅ zín-(pa) tsam ɣda (btang na nam mkha'i bya yang zin pa tsam gda') if I let him loose, he might almost catch a bird in the air, = zín-pa daṅ ₀drao (zin pa dang 'dra'o) Mil.; with a partic.: rtags yód-pa tsám-la (rtags yod pa tsam la) = rtags daṅ yód-pa-la (rtags dang yod pa la) to every one that has the mark Glr.; rtsa daṅ rús-pa tsam (rtsa dang rus pa tsam) Dzl. nothing but skin and bones; ₀gro mi nús-pa ₀góg-pa tsam ('gro mi nus pa 'gog pa tsam) Dzl. one only creeping, not being able to walk; ča tsam šes kyaṅ (ca tsam shes kyang) if one knows but a particle, but a little bit; sems tsám-mo (sems tsam mo) they exist only in our fancy Was.; tsigs-ma tsam yód-dam (tshigs ma tsam yod dam) Dzl. is not the sediment at least still left? lḥág-ma tsam žig (lhag ma tsam zhig) Dzl. but a remnant; brgya tsam (brgya tsam) may mean: about one hundred, or: only one hundred; in some cases tsam (tsam) is untranslatable: lṅa-brgyá tsam tams-čád tsei dus byas-so (lnga brgya tsam thams cad tshe'i dus byas so) the 500 merchants died all Dzl. (15, 9 s.l. c.); bdén-pa tsam yod (bden pa tsam yod) Mil. some grain of truth is in the matter; tsig daṅ rnám-par ₀drä-ba tsam ₀dúg-na-₀aṅ (tshig dang rnam par 'dra ba tsam 'dug na 'ang) Mil. though it is all but equal to the words, i.e. very much like the real tenor or wording; it may also be combined with
